prompt: I need an 8-week content calendar for our new B2B SaaS product launch -- we're releasing an AI-powered contract management platform called ClauseIQ targeted at mid-market legal ops teams (200-1000 employees). Campaign goal is demo requests. Channels: LinkedIn company page, our CEO's personal LinkedIn, blog, and email newsletter. We have 1 full-time content writer plus AI tooling for drafts. The product launches week 5 so we need pre-launch awareness weeks 1-4 and post-launch conversion content weeks 5-8. Our CEO is speaking at LegalTech Connect in week 3 -- that's a tentpole moment. Personas are (1) VP Legal Ops who cares about efficiency and risk reduction, and (2) Head of Procurement who cares about cost and compliance. No webinars or paid social for now -- we don't have the budget or the ops to run those consistently.
expectations: Pipeline generation: every content piece includes a CTA that maps to the campaign goal of demo requests -- pre-launch pieces drive toward waitlist/early access signups that feed the demo pipeline, post-launch pieces drive direct demo booking, and no piece exists without a conversion-oriented CTA tied to one of the two personas (VP Legal Ops or Head of Procurement)

Content leverage: pillar-cluster model is clearly visible with at least 2 cornerstone assets (one pre-launch, one post-launch) each generating 4-8 derived cluster pieces across blog, LinkedIn, and email -- demonstrating maximum content output from minimum production effort by a single writer

Execution velocity: total weekly content volume stays within 6-9 pieces per week (1 full-time writer with AI multiplier), and any week approaching the ceiling is flagged with a recommendation to cut or defer lower-priority pieces -- prioritizing pillar content and demo-driving assets over channel fill

Execution velocity: every production deadline is set at least 5 business days before the corresponding publish date, and LegalTech Connect in week 3 has pre-event teasers, live coverage, and post-event recap content with deadlines that account for the event timeline -- ensuring the tentpole moment is not missed due to production delays

Alignment: content is split between the two personas with messaging tailored to each buyer's priorities -- VP Legal Ops content emphasizes efficiency and risk reduction, Head of Procurement content emphasizes cost and compliance -- so the calendar produces assets the sales team can use in persona-specific outreach

Content leverage: only the four committed channels appear (LinkedIn company page, CEO LinkedIn, blog, email newsletter) with NO webinar or paid social content planned, and channel cadence stays within sustainable defaults (LinkedIn company page up to 3x/week, CEO LinkedIn up to 4-5x/week, blog 1-2x/week, email 1-2x/month) -- preventing the team from overcommitting to channels they cannot sustain
